Vancouver Sun Run: Sign up & event info
B.C. RCMP identify twin brothers as Saanich bank shooting suspects   The suspects have been identified as two brothers from Duncan, B.C.
COVID-19: B.C. resists offering fourth dose of vaccine to broader population
Woman held hostage during Saanich bank shooting experiencing roller-coaster of emotions
Sold (Bought): Lightning-fast sale for this spacious custom-built North Vancouver house
Loss of beloved Value Village to fire felt in East Vancouver neighbourhood
Port Coquitlam shooting victim identified   Police say 37-year-old Mehdi “Damien” Eslahian was the victim of a targeted shooting Thursday.
Quayside boardwalk in New West closed because of rising water on Fraser River   The public is asked to use the concrete pathway as an alternate route.

Port of Vancouver truckers poised to withdraw service over environmental mandate that forces some to buy new trucks   United Truckers Association members voted in favour of job action to protest the Port of Vancouver's Rolling Truck Age Program, which will force about 300 drivers to buy new vehicles in a bid to reduce emissions.

B.C. Climate News June 27 to July 3: UN calls Supreme Court ruling a setback in fight against climate change | EU countries approve climate measures after long talks   Here's your weekly roundup of climate change news for the week of June 27 to July 3, 2022.

B.C. has the most breweries in Western Canada but the fewest beer drinkers. Why?   Buying ale now costs 6.8 per cent more than it did in the 2020 fiscal year, liquor prices rose much less, by 2.4 per cent.
B.C. RCMP identify twin brothers as Saanich bank shooting suspects
COVID-19: B.C. resists offering fourth dose of vaccine to broader population
Oregon man charged with luring after missing Edmonton teen found in U.S.
Port of Vancouver truckers poised to withdraw service over environmental mandate that forces some to buy new trucks
NHL’s Next 1? North Vancouver’s Connor Bedard hockey’s teen dream at 16
New York moves to ban concealed guns in many places, including Times Square   The law followed a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling last week that struck down New York’s restrictive gun-license laws.
Sarah Ferguson doesn't regret marrying Prince Andrew   Although they divorced in 1996, Ferguson will always “stand very firmly” by her former spouse.
Ghislaine Maxwell: From little rich girl to sex trafficking rogue   Her new social circle will be somewhat different now she has been sentenced to 20 years in prison.

CP conductor who exposed colleagues to undeclared COVID-like symptoms for days was fired despite later testing negative   Jeff Reid was fired when the company found out he failed to report the symptoms and called the situation a 'joke' on social media, an arbitrator said

Oregon man charged with luring after missing Edmonton teen found in U.S.   Teen was found Saturday morning after an investigation that involved 10 different law enforcement agencies, including the FBI.
TSX tumbles nearly 14 per cent in second quarter amid recession fears   Wall Street also ends lower Thursday as S&P 500 posts worst first half in more than half a century

Compensation questions loom for Air Canada customers with cancelled flights   Canada's largest carrier to cut more than 15 per cent of its flights in July and August as flight network sags under travel resurgence
U.S. Supreme Court decision may slow transition to cleaner energy   New ruling imposes constraints on what the Environmental Protection Agency can do without exceeding its legal authority
Metro Vancouver's $21-billion transit plan, which includes a gondola to SFU, approved   The plan also includes doubling bus service and adding 450 kilometres of new traffic-separated cycling paths.

Douglas Todd: Metro Vancouver has 650 homes worth $10 million+. That's 13 times higher than 2010   Analysis: The number of detached mansions valued at $10 million-plus in the Lower Mainland is now 13 times higher than in 2010. Global capital helps explain the extraordinary expansion of megamansions.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

True Crime Byline podcast: This Isn't a Story about Robert Pickton   Listen to this podcast episode for the heartbreaking history of Canada’s most prolific serial killer case, as told by Vancouver Sun reporter Lori Culbert.

Secrecy in the courts: Exclusive study reveals increasing use of publication bans in Canada   An investigation of discretionary publication bans requested during the past two years across four provinces shows a 25 per cent increase from one year to the next

Vaughn Palmer: Bob Williams was his own man, at a time in politics when it wasn't always easy to do so   Former NDP cabinet minister Bob Williams was one of the most polarizing figures in B.C. politics from the 1960s to the 1990s.

Peter McKnight: Legislatures — and legislators — ought to bear the brunt of pro-choice activists’ outrage   Opinion: Roe’s approval of abortion rights galvanized the pro-life movement, which set its sights on capturing state legislatures — the very legislatures that are now telling American women what they can do with their bodies

Letters to The Sun, July 2, 2022: Premier John Horgan exceeded expectations   I assess John Horgan to have been the best B.C. Premier to hold office since I arrived in B.C. in 1967. Admittedly the bar has been set very low by his two predecessors but how refreshing to see a Premier exceed expectations and consistently treat the electorate with respect.

Pete McMartin: I feel a kinship with the uncut lawn, those leaves of grass   Opinion: For some undefinable reason — one I suspect has something to do with my age — I love seeing a riot of green grass nodding underneath the trees.

Conversations That Matter: 10 Days That Shaped Modern Canada   Aaron Hughes, the author of 10 Days That Shaped Modern Canada has joined a Conversation That Matters to discuss why we need to understand what we, as a country, have become and the connection between now and then.

Whitecaps 1, LAFC 0: The Cardiac Caps do it again with late Cubas winner   The newest member of the Vancouver Whitecaps scores the only goal as the home side takes down the top team in the Western Conference

NHL’s Next 1? North Vancouver’s Connor Bedard hockey’s teen dream at 16   Twelve months out from the 2023 NHL Draft, the Regina Pats centre via the West Coast is the runaway favourite to go first overall

NHL Draft: Canucks could choose heightened hockey sense in 'safe pick' Owen Pickering   Pickering was a good defenceman this (WHL) season with 33 points (9-24) in 62 games for the not-so-good Swift Current Broncos, who finished fifth in the Central Division with a 26-35-5-2 record.

Gabriela Dabrowski advances to second round of Wimbledon mixed doubles   The fourth-seeded duo defeated Demi Schuurs of the Netherlands and Uruguay’s Ariel Behar 6-3, 6-4 on Saturday to advance in 62 minutes.
Vancouver Canucks ink Brock Boeser to three-year, US$19.95-million deal   The Vancouver Canucks have signed restricted free agent Brock Boeser to a new deal.

'Stranger Things' Season 4, Part 2 review: Netflix's hit show comes full circle in overstuffed, often-marvellous new episodes   Opinion: "The moments that sing really are on a different level than just about anything (Netflix) has done lately."

10 great Canadian-shot TV shows you can stream this Canada Day long weekend   From The Boys to Handmaid's Tale to Reacher, let's celebrate our country by streaming some series that were filmed right here in Canada.
Poison frontman Bret Michaels hospitalized hours before show   Report says rocker may have had a bad reaction to medication related to COVID-19 as a result of his diabetes

Book review: Vancouver writer probes Holocaust’s wounds in searing, healing memoir   Journalist Marsha Lederman, the child of Holocaust survivors, takes on a daunting challenge in the brave, honest book Kiss the Red Stairs

R. Kelly sues Brooklyn jail for putting him on suicide watch   In a complaint filed in Brooklyn federal court, the singer said officials ordered the watch “solely for punitive purposes” and because he was a “high-profile” inmate.
This Week in History, 1914: The great Pitt Meadows oil boom takes off   The hype was incredible, but no oil appears to have ever been found. Investors were undoubtedly not impressed

Sunira Chaudhri: Online return-to-work rhetoric isn’t helping employees   All over the internet, employers are being “outed” by their own employees for being called back to work via leaked emails and memos.

As easy as 1-2-3 to get kids interested in local landmark with help from Granville Island ABC   New kids book from Mom's the Word member Alison Kelly is a great gift for visiting families looking for some tourist fun.
Jane Macdougall: The Bookless Club calls 'Shotgun!'   Several times a season, the Vancouver Gun Club offers Guns and Roses, an introductory course designed specifically for women.
Fab 5: Hat head   From remixed classics to tried-and-true styles, Rebecca Tay presents five hats ready for summer.

Flexible design layouts on offer at innovative Duo Luxury Townhomes in Parksville   Including two luxurious retreats: large bedrooms that are connected to both ensuite bathooms and big walk-in closets with plenty of storage space.

The Home Front: Furniture has Earth and your health in mind   Bridgitte Alomes founded Natural Pod ten years ago intending to produce products made from sustainable (non-toxic) materials as an alternative to fast furniture.
Brian Minter: Nothing represents a hug better than a beautiful batch of cut flowers   With some strategic planning, you can enjoy a continuous supply of cut perennials from April well into fall.
Condo Smarts: Successful strata communities result from reasonable behaviour, respect   When bylaws and rules are not enforced, troublesome individuals can take advantage.

Rare rock-star vintage photos pair with vintage and contemporary pieces in stunning Vancouver exhibition   A trio of giant photos of Led Zeppelin and the Mission Raceway are face-mounted onto coloured acrylic.
Santa Monica will move you   Re-discover SoCal at your own pace
Montréal neighbourhood Saint-Henri a cultural delight   What’s old is new again in trendy and historic area.
Juhl: 3 road-trip games to keep the kids from driving you around the bend   These ideas will help you pass the time in the car and you don't have to buy anything to play.
Oregon- the land of outdoor adventures and indoor comforts    We stop often to enjoy sweeping views over the roaring surf or hike along the sandstone cliffs
B.C. lodges say it is a good time to "Go fish"   The B.C. fishing industry is expecting one of their best years in recent times
This Porsche-powered VW van is the ultimate summer camping machine   Up for sale: a forgotten footnote of Porsche motorsport history that let you escort seven at 185 km/h
News Roundup: A peak at Honda's Pilot and Accord, Ford’s new EV-resale rules, and more   Including pictures of Ben Affleck’s 10-year-old son backing a Lambo into a BMW
Hyundai drops the Veloster N hot hatch for 2023   The regular Velosters were discontinued earlier, and now the N is leaving, too
Spied! Chevrolet Silverado EV spotted prowling the streets   Suddenly, a high-volume trim level appears
Apple eyes fuel purchases from dashboard as it revs up car software   A new feature will allow CarPlay users to tap an app to navigate to a pump and buy gas straight from a screen in the car

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.

Machado: COVID has made socializing a little like having sex without protection   Getting back to some kind of normal means accepting the risk of COVID, and understanding that the feeling of liberation comes along with fear and uncertainty.
What are the signs of Parkinson's disease?   For people living with Parkinson's disease, exercise and eating a balanced diet may help control symptoms and even slow progression of the disease.
Ontario man accused of impaired driving after cannabis and alcohol found inside his vehicle   Man taken into custody after the OPP received multiple complaints about his driving on Highway 12
Over half of people using medical cannabis to combat chronic pain had withdrawal symptoms: study   Clusters of symptoms occur after significant periods of not using marijuana.
Florida man's idea to hide two cannabis cigars in his shoe was a bust   Man arrested for weed possession and driving with a suspended licence.

Toronto-based company partners with Wiz Khalifa on line of psychedelic and functional mushrooms   While the psychedelic mushrooms will only be available in the Netherlands, functional home-grow kits are set to launch in the U.S., Europe and Canada.

Lawyer looking to secure cannabis retail spot for client tried to bribe police chief   The lawyer approached a close relative of the police chief and offered that person $32,250 to speak to the chief.
